kpis.label: Policy rate after the June 16, 2026 hike — highest since 1995
Since exit from easing (Mar 2024): Jul '24, Jan '25, Dec '25, Jun '26
Next MPM (2026) — decision Jul 31 with a new Outlook Report; first vote for Sato
Net board drift: dovish dissenters replaced; doves now accept gradual hikes
Asada (PM Takaichi's first pick) voted to hold in June 2026 — a new dovish pole

speakers.name: UEDA Kazuo
speakers.quote: "If upside risks to prices outweigh downside risks to economic activity, it would be necessary to thoroughly discuss raising the policy interest rate." — Jun 2026

remarks.source: Meeting with PM Takaichi at PM's Office
remarks.comment: Following talks with PM Takaichi at the PM's Office, Ueda said he made no specific requests — only a general discussion of economic and financial conditions.

remarks.source: Speech read by Dep Gov Himino (Ueda absent)
remarks.comment: In a speech read by Deputy Governor Himino (Ueda was absent), Ueda signalled caution on the Middle East conflict's impact, keeping policy options open and cooling expectations of an April rate hike.
Ueda disclosed that PM Takaichi asked the BOJ to conduct "appropriate policy" while taking into consideration the government's price measures, in line with the joint government-BOJ accord.

At an international banking conference in Tokyo, Ueda warned that oil price shocks are "tests of the entire inflation regime," flagging the need for vigilance without giving a clear hint on the June policy meeting outcome.

Ueda signalled a good chance of a June rate hike, stating that if upside risks to prices outweigh downside risks to economic activity, it would be necessary to "thoroughly discuss the pros and cons of raising the policy interest rate."
In a speech, Ueda reiterated that the BOJ sees upside risks to its 2% inflation target and will conduct additional rate hikes "appropriately," reaffirming the bank's tightening bias.

speakers.name: TAMURA Naoki
speakers.shift: Consistently the board's most hawkish voice (≈ +2 throughout)
speakers.quote: "It is quite possible that, as early as this spring, the price stability target... can be judged to have been achieved"; hike impact so far "extremely limited"; neutral rate "at least around 1 percent." — Kanagawa, Feb 13, 2026.

remarks.source: Speech — Meeting with Local Businesses, Hyogo
remarks.comment: The baseline path is to raise the interest rate by a quarter percentage point at intervals of a few months toward the neutral interest rate level of 2%; the BOJ needs to raise rates every few months and should quicken the pace given stronger inflation risks. No rapid fire hikes are needed now. Need to hike rates to neutral to avoid an overshoot. Should search for the neutral level while hiking rates, but it's hard to pinpoint the level of neutral rate.

speakers.name: TAKATA Hajime
speakers.shift: "Gear shift" advocate → proposed 1.0% hike at Jan 2026 MPM (≈ +1.5 → +2)
speakers.quote: "At the January 2026 MPM, I proposed that the Bank raise the policy interest rate to 1.0 percent as a further gear shift." — Kyoto, Feb 26, 2026.

remarks.comment: Delivered hawkish remarks, warning that a global recovery and rate hikes could lead to the risk of unintentionally falling behind the curve.

speakers.name: HIMINO Ryozo
speakers.shift: Steady gradualist; intellectual groundwork for hikes (≈ +0.5 → +1)
speakers.quote: Mar 2026: policy "should gradually shift to a more neutral stance through moderate policy rate hikes."

remarks.source: Speech to business leaders, Wakayama; press conference
remarks.comment: The BOJ should gradually shift to a more neutral stance through moderate policy rate hikes. Financial conditions appear still accommodative and the impact of rate hikes has been limited so far; there is a lag before data show the effect of rate hikes.

Weaker yen is a factor pushing up CPI via higher import costs and corporate price-passing moves. Monetary policy is not targeted at guiding or controlling FX rate levels, but rather at achieving price stability. Japan's economy is in an inflationary situation.
The BOJ is at the stage of watching the risk of economic slowdown and inflation in the case of prolonged escalation of the Middle East situation. Does not see Japan as being in stagflation now.

It is important for the BOJ to maintain market trust in controlling inflation by adjusting the degree of monetary easing appropriately. The BOJ will raise rates in response to economic and price developments; timing and pace will depend on the economic impact of the Middle East conflict. Appropriate policy adjustments are key to keeping market confidence amid the recent JGB selloff.

Sees a risk that inflation could accelerate beyond the 2% target; pass-through of higher costs in B2B transactions is proceeding at a somewhat faster pace and could eventually spread to broader price increases. Delay in rate hikes might end up weighing on the economy. Higher corporate prices expected to start affecting CPI soon; FX fluctuations more likely to affect prices.

speakers.name: UCHIDA Shinichi
speakers.quote: After the Aug 2024 rout, pledged no hikes amid market instability. Fronted the June 2026 presser during Ueda's hospitalization.

remarks.source: BOJ Post-Meeting Press Conference (hosted for hospitalized Governor Ueda)
remarks.comment: Policymakers will conduct policy so as to avoid falling behind the curve; BOJ is watching not only real interest rates but also overall conditions. Even with the rate hike, financial conditions remain accommodative and the policy rate continues to support the economy. It has become a trend that wages and prices rise together, which is a positive factor for the economy. Yen depreciation is increasingly influencing price trends, making it a crucial part of monetary policy decision-making. There is a substantial range in neutral rate estimates, making it hard to specify in practice; without knowing exactly what the neutral rate is, it is hard to know how much more the BOJ has to raise rates.

speakers.name: KOEDA Junko
speakers.shift: Data-driven, leans hawkish from arrival (≈ +1); replaced dove Adachi
speakers.quote: "Underlying inflation is about 2 percent"; "the Bank needs to proceed with interest rate normalization... to avoid creating unintended distortions." — Niigata, Nov 20, 2025.

remarks.source: Speech — Meeting with Local Business Leaders, Fukuoka + Post-Speech Press Briefing
remarks.comment: Underlying inflation is already around 2%; sees some possibility that underlying inflation may exceed 2% looking ahead given the Middle East situation. It is reasonable for the BOJ to raise the policy interest rate at an appropriate pace to address high inflation while considering trade-offs for the economy. The role of monetary policy in responding to inflation is increasing. Authorities continue to monitor upside risks for prices and downside risks for the economy; sees bigger risks for prices. BOJ will set monetary policy in a manner that ensures it won't fall behind the curve in fighting inflation. Notes that the lower band of the neutral rate for Japan is around 1%.

speakers.name: MASU Kazuyuki
speakers.shift: Supports normalization, stresses caution (≈ +0.5); replaced dove Nakamura
speakers.quote: "I am convinced that continuing with further policy interest rate hikes will be needed to complete the normalization"... but the Bank will "proceed cautiously." — Ehime, Feb 6, 2026.

remarks.source: Speech — Meeting with Local Business Leaders
remarks.comment: BOJ needs to continue raising its benchmark interest rate to complete the process of policy normalization; this would help end the situation where Japan and other countries are pursuing contrasting monetary policy directions. Although underlying inflation remains below 2%, it is on a rising trend. Not particularly desirable if the price trend stays above the 2% target. Does not have any specific pace in mind for when the BOJ should raise rates; will monitor economic data carefully when making policy decisions; the central bank won't hesitate to move its policy rate when convinced by examining economic data.

If statistical data do not indicate clear signs of an economic downturn, it is desirable to raise the policy rate at the earliest stage possible. Further rate hikes are needed to complete policy normalization.

speakers.name: ASADA Toichiro
speakers.role: Board Member (from Apr 2026; PM Takaichi's first pick)
speakers.shift: Sole dissenter at the June 2026 hike (≈ −1.5)
speakers.quote: Any further hike conditional on the economy's “endogenous strength” — wage- and demand-driven inflation toward the 2% target, with employment weighed alongside prices (Reuters, Jul 8 2026). Jul 7 (Sankei): raising rates could slow the economy. Sole dissenter at the June 2026 hike — preferred to hold at 0.75%, citing downside risks to production and employment.

remarks.comment: Struck a neutral tone, stating he would "carefully examine the various data and information before making a judgment". On exchange rates, said "I cannot say whether yen weakness is desirable or yen strength is desirable."

Cast the sole dissenting vote against the BOJ's rate hike (0.75% → 1.0%), arguing downside risks to production and employment outweighed upside risks to prices. Preferred to maintain the existing guideline for money market operations.

Reportedly pointed out that the Japanese economy could return to deflation in a worst-case scenario, emphasising downside risks to production and employment in contrast to the prevailing hawkish tone among most board members.
Said that raising interest rates could lead to a slowdown in the economy.

Stated that any further rate hikes would be conditional on confirming the economy's "endogenous strength" — wage- and demand-driven inflation toward the 2% target. Also indicated that employment would be considered alongside inflation in policy decisions.

speakers.name: SATO Ayano
speakers.quote: "Inflation is not that strong by societal norms." / "Need to watch for both downside risks to growth and upside risks to inflation." — Inaugural press conference, Jun 30, 2026

remarks.source: First Press Conference as BOJ Policymaker
remarks.comment: There is some evidence emerging that suggests currency fluctuations are having a larger impact on prices than before.

Accommodative monetary conditions exist if the policy rate is below the neutral rate, restrictive if above; noted the neutral rate is not directly observable. Found it difficult to definitively state whether upside risks to prices or downside risks to the economy were greater — a stance considered slightly more dovish than Governor Ueda's June 3 speech. FX movements could affect underlying inflation with both positive and negative effects, but preferred not to comment further in her capacity as a Board member. Did not offer specific views on monetary conditions or economic risks, avoided mentioning exchange rates, and did not express definite views on rate hikes or suggest she was a reflationist.

board.record: Chairman; in the majority at all meetings. Absent (hospitalized) for the Jun 2026 vote.
Voted with the majority throughout; fronted Jun 2026 presser.

remarks.source: BOJ Post-Meeting Press Conference (hosted for hospitalized Governor Ueda)
remarks.comment: Policymakers will conduct policy so as to avoid falling behind the curve; BOJ is watching not only real interest rates but also overall conditions. Even with the rate hike, financial conditions remain accommodative and the policy rate continues to support the economy. It has become a trend that wages and prices rise together, which is a positive factor for the economy. There is a substantial range in neutral rate estimates, making it hard to specify in practice; without knowing exactly what the neutral rate is, it is hard to know how much more the BOJ has to raise rates.

board.record: Dec 2024: proposed 0.5% · Jun 2025: opposed slowing the JGB taper · Sep & Oct 2025: proposed 0.75%.

remarks.source: Speech — Meeting with Local Businesses, Hyogo
remarks.comment: The baseline path is to raise the interest rate by a quarter percentage point at intervals of a few months toward the neutral interest rate level of 2%; the BOJ needs to raise rates every few months and should quicken the pace given stronger inflation risks. No rapid fire hikes are needed now. Need to hike rates to neutral to avoid an overshoot. Should search for the neutral level while hiking rates, but it's hard to pinpoint the level of neutral rate.

board.record: Sep & Oct 2025: proposed 0.75% · Jan 2026: proposed 1.0% · Mar 2026: proposed a hike.

remarks.comment: Delivered hawkish remarks, warning that a global recovery and rate hikes could lead to the risk of unintentionally falling behind the curve.

board.record: Voted with the majority since joining.

remarks.source: Speech — Meeting with Local Business Leaders, Fukuoka + Post-Speech Press Briefing
remarks.comment: Underlying inflation is already around 2%; sees some possibility that underlying inflation may exceed 2% looking ahead given the Middle East situation. board.record: Jun 2026: sole dissent against hike to 1.00% (preferred to hold).
Joined end-June 2026 (replaced Nakagawa); no votes yet. Reported reflationist lean.

remarks.source: First Press Conference as BOJ Policymaker
remarks.comment: There is some evidence emerging that suggests currency fluctuations are having a larger impact on prices than before.

Accommodative monetary conditions exist if the policy rate is below the neutral rate, restrictive if above; noted the neutral rate is not directly observable. Found it difficult to definitively state whether upside risks to prices or downside risks to the economy were greater — a stance considered slightly more dovish than Governor Ueda's June 3 speech. FX movements could affect underlying inflation with both positive and negative effects, but preferred not to comment further in her capacity as a Board member. Did not offer specific views on monetary conditions or economic risks, avoided mentioning exchange rates, and did not express definite views on rate hikes or suggest she was a reflationist.

board.record: No formal dissents; reportedly urged a hike at the Apr 2026 hold.
Jul 2024: dissented against the hike to 0.25%.
Jul 2024 and Jan 2025: dissented against the hikes.
Dovish rhetoric but voted with the majority in window.

timeline.text: Kuroda launches QQE ("bazooka"): 2% inflation target, JGB purchases doubled to ¥50tn/yr, monetary base targeted to double in 2 years.
NIRP introduced: BOJ adopts −0.1% policy rate on excess reserves — first negative rate in BOJ history.
YCC introduced: BOJ targets 10-year JGB yield at ~0% alongside −0.1% short rate; marks shift from quantity- to rate-based QE.
YCC band shock-widened from ±0.25% to ±0.5%: surprise move under Kuroda, widely seen as de facto tightening; 10Y yield surges.
YCC upper limit raised to 1.0% (reference point, not hard cap): Ueda's first major tweak; 10Y yield allowed to drift toward 1%.
Historic pivot: NIRP ended (rate +0→0.1%), YCC formally scrapped, ETF new purchases halted — first rate hike since 2007 and end of 8 years of negative rates.
Rate raised to 0.25%; JGB purchase-reduction plan announced.
Global market rout; Deputy Governor Uchida calms markets — no hikes while markets are unstable.
Rate raised to 0.5% (highest since 2008), after Himino's and Ueda's signaling.
US tariff shock: Outlook Report cuts FY25 growth forecast from 1.1% to 0.5%; hiking cycle pauses.
JGB taper extended at gentler pace from Apr 2026; ETF/J-REIT sales decided (Sep). Jul: Japan–US trade agreement.
Holds, but Tamura and Takata dissent at both meetings, proposing 0.75%.
Takaichi government takes office; Oct Outlook: FY25 CPI 2.7%, FY26 1.8%, FY27 2.0%.
Rate raised to 0.75% (unanimous) — first hike in 11 months; 10-year JGB yield tops 2%.
Hold, but Takata proposes going to 1.0%. Jan Outlook restores pre-tariff forecasts.

US–Japan rate check (yen): after the BOJ held at 0.75%, an unusual rate check by the NY Fed — echoed by finance officials in Tokyo — spiked the yen ~1.7% to ¥155.6, its biggest one-day jump since Aug and the year's strongest level. First sign of possible coordinated US–Japan action to defend the yen; ¥160 seen as the red line. MOF data (Jan 30) confirmed no actual intervention followed — verbal/rate-check warnings only.
Hold at 0.75% amid Iran-war worries; inflation forecast raised. Nakagawa reportedly calls for a hike.
Rate raised to 1.00% (7–1; Asada dissents), highest since Sep 1995, amid yen weakness and FX intervention.

mpm_calendar.notes: Next meeting. First vote for Sato Ayano; first meeting with the rate at 1.00%.
Released immediately after the MPM; full text 2pm next business day.
Full record of the 7–1 hike to 1.00%, incl. Asada's dissent.
No Outlook Report at this meeting.
Final meeting of 2026; no Outlook Report.

July 2026.context: Super-long supply amid post-hike curve repricing
Prices in the July MPM outcome
MPM day 1 — short end most exposed to the Jul 31 decision

August 2026.context: First 10y supply after the July MPM/Outlook
Breakeven read with CPI still near 2%+

fiscal.relevance: Revenue outturn frames the scale of any autumn supplementary budget.

fiscal.event: Ministries' FY2027 budget requests submitted to MOF
fiscal.relevance: First indication of spending pressure under the Takaichi administration.
A package on the scale of the 2025 'Comprehensive Economic Measures' would imply additional JGB issuance.

fiscal.event: Ruling-party tax reform outline for FY2027
fiscal.relevance: Sets the revenue side of the budget; signals the fiscal stance.

fiscal.event: FY2027 draft budget & FY2027 JGB Issuance Plan (cabinet approval)
fiscal.relevance: Determines gross issuance and maturity mix; coincides with BOJ deliberations on its post-Apr 2027 purchase plan — fiscal and monetary calendars intersect.
Budget deliberations begin; political friction can delay passage.
Required before the fiscal year starts April 1.

fiscal.event: Basic Policy on Economic and Fiscal Management ('honebuto')
fiscal.relevance: Sets the medium-term fiscal framework, incl. the primary-balance target.

fiscal.event: MOF meetings with JGB Market Special Participants / investors
fiscal.relevance: Typically precede issuance-mix changes; moved markets during the 2025 super-long selloff.
Sustained stimulus raises the salience of rating commentary for the long end.

press_conferences.note: Ueda absent (hospitalised). Presser fronted by Uchida.
